Pakistan Districts: Choropleth

Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi

2026-07-17

library(tidyverse)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

if (!requireNamespace("sf", quietly = TRUE)) {
  stop("The 'sf' package is required for the choropleth map. ",
       "Run renv::restore() from the project root, then re-render ",
       "(sf also needs system GDAL/GEOS/PROJ).",
       call. = FALSE)
}
suppressMessages(library(sf))   # attach so geom_sf + st_as_sf dispatch

# 1. Load the metric data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate that
#    every *_var param names a real DATA column.
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# Resolve the spatial assets (bare filenames -> absolute paths under 1_data/geo/)
geo_path    <- here::here("1_data", "geo", params$geo_file)
lookup_path <- here::here("1_data", "geo", params$lookup_file)

# 2. Load the geometry and confirm the join key exists inside it.
if (!file.exists(geo_path)) {
  stop("Geometry file not found: ", geo_path,
       "\nCheck the 'geo_file' parameter.", call. = FALSE)
}
geo <- sf::st_read(geo_path, quiet = TRUE)
if (!(params$geo_key %in% names(geo))) {
  stop("geo_key '", params$geo_key, "' is not a field in the geometry. ",
       "Fields available: ", paste(setdiff(names(geo), "geometry"), collapse = " | "),
       call. = FALSE)
}

# 3. Load the versioned crosswalk (skips its leading '#' comment header).
lookup <- NULL
if (file.exists(lookup_path)) {
  lookup <- readr::read_csv(lookup_path, comment = "#",
                            show_col_types = FALSE, trim_ws = TRUE)
  names(lookup) <- stringr::str_squish(names(lookup))
}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.6_viz_functions.R"), local = TRUE)

# Thin wrapper: name harmonization, match-based attach, quantile binning + the
# map itself all live in viz_choropleth(); the wrapper hands it the loaded
# geometry and crosswalk. Empty-string text params keep the auto defaults.
viz_choropleth(
  data           = raw_data,
  geo            = geo,
  lookup         = lookup,
  region_var     = params$region_var,
  value_var      = params$value_var,
  geo_key        = params$geo_key,
  n_bins         = params$n_bins,
  metric_name    = params$metric_name,
  chart_title    = params$chart_title,
  chart_subtitle = params$chart_subtitle,
  source_note    = params$source_note
)
